info.title: Valar API
info.description: Valar exposes Responses and Chat Completions endpoints that match the OpenAI shape. This reference documents the fields Valar supports today.

tags.name: Models API
tags.description: Discover which models you can call.

tags.name: Responses API
tags.description: Responses endpoints that follow the OpenAI interface.

tags.name: Chat Completions API
tags.description: Chat Completions endpoints that follow the OpenAI interface.

tags.name: Batches API
tags.description: Submit request batches and track their progress.

RequestMetadata.description: Optional string-valued metadata. Use completion_window to influence scheduling, and completion_webhook together with webhook_token to wire up a webhook that fires on completion.

completion_window.description: The [completion window](/inference-modes#completion-windows), or latency tier, you want for this request. `asap` is the on-demand **Now** tier; `standard` is the lower-cost default. Check the [Pricing](/pricing) page to see which windows each model supports. If you leave it out, Valar picks `standard` when the model supports it and falls back to `asap` otherwise. See [default behavior](/inference-modes#default-behavior).

ResponseInputImagePart.description: An image content part. You can only send image input to multimodal models; check the Models page to see which ones qualify.

image_url.description: Either a publicly reachable http(s) URL or a base64 data URI in the form data:<media-type>;base64,<data>.

ResponseInput.description: Accepts text, and image input (input_image) when you target a multimodal model. Valar does not yet accept audio, files, or item references.

prompt_cache_key.description: An optional hint Valar uses to keep prompt-prefix caches local. Requests sharing a key are steered toward the same place so you hit the cache more often.

store.description: Must be true; no other value is accepted.

stream.description: Streaming is not available on this endpoint yet.

ChatImageContentPart.description: An image content part. You can only send image input to multimodal models; check the Models page to see which ones qualify.

url.description: Either a publicly reachable http(s) URL or a base64 data URI.

n.description: Must be 1; requesting multiple choices is not supported yet.

stream.description: Set this to true to receive the answer as a Server-Sent Events stream of chat.completion.chunk objects rather than one JSON payload.

stream_options.description: Settings that only take effect while streaming (stream is true).

include_usage.description: Set this to true to receive one final chunk carrying token usage for the whole request, sent just ahead of the [DONE] terminator.

store.description: Must be true; no other value is accepted.

ChatCompletionChunk.description: A single Server-Sent Events chunk produced while stream is true. The full streamed reply arrives as a series of these chat.completion.chunk objects, each delivered on its own SSE data: line, with a closing data: [DONE] line at the end.

custom_id.description: Your own identifier for this request, unique within the batch. Use lowercase letters and digits, optionally with hyphens and underscores, between 1 and 64 characters.

params.description: The parameters for this request, using the same fields you would send in a Responses API request body.

endpoint.description: Which API endpoint every request in the batch targets. Right now /v1/responses is the only valid choice.

requests.description: The requests that make up the batch, from 1 up to 100,000 of them. The combined request body has to stay under 256 MB.

label.description: An optional name for the batch. Use letters and digits, optionally with hyphens and underscores, up to 128 characters.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Lets you retry safely. Valar records a reservation under the combination of organization, API key, and Idempotency-Key, so sending the same value again hands back the stored response rather than running inference a second time. Values can be up to 255 characters. See [Idempotent Requests](/idempotency) for the complete rules.

AnthropicIdempotencyKey.name: anthropic-idempotency-key
AnthropicIdempotencyKey.description: The Anthropic-style idempotency header. It behaves exactly like Idempotency-Key and exists on the /messages endpoint so you can keep to Anthropic's naming.

get.summary: List the models you can call

401.description: We could not authenticate the request.

post.description: Starts a Responses API task in the OpenAI style. You get back 202 when you pass background=true; otherwise the call returns 200 once the work finishes.

value.input: Explain the key ideas behind transformer architectures.

200.description: The response finished and is returned to you right away.

202.description: The response was queued and will be processed in the background.

400.description: Your request was malformed or asked for something Valar does not support.

401.description: We could not authenticate the request.

500.description: Something went wrong on Valar's side.

502.description: An upstream service returned an error.

504.description: The request timed out before the result was ready.

401.description: We could not authenticate the request.

404.description: No response exists for that ID.

500.description: Something went wrong on Valar's side.

post.description: A Chat Completions endpoint that follows the OpenAI interface. Pass stream: true to stream the reply back as Server-Sent Events made up of chat.completion.chunk objects.

200.description: The chat completion. By default this is one JSON object; with stream: true it becomes a Server-Sent Events stream of chat.completion.chunk objects closed by a final data: [DONE] line.

400.description: Your request was malformed or asked for something Valar does not support.

401.description: We could not authenticate the request.

408.description: The request timed out before the result was ready.

500.description: Something went wrong on Valar's side.

post.description: Send a group of requests for Valar to process in the background.

request_counts.description: How many requests the batch contains.

created_at.description: When the batch was created, as a Unix timestamp.

label.description: The label you gave the batch, when one was set.

401.description: We could not authenticate the request.

500.description: Something went wrong on Valar's side.

get.description: Retrieve your batches, paging through them when you need to.

parameters.name: after_id
parameters.description: Only return batches created after this batch ID.

parameters.name: before_id
parameters.description: Only return batches created before this batch ID.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: How many batches to return at most. Ranges from 1 to 200 and defaults to 25.

request_counts.description: How many requests the batch contains.

created_at.description: When the batch was created, as a Unix timestamp.

label.description: The label you gave the batch, when one was set.

401.description: We could not authenticate the request.

500.description: Something went wrong on Valar's side.

get.description: See where a batch currently stands.

request_counts.description: How many requests the batch contains.

created_at.description: When the batch was created, as a Unix timestamp.

label.description: The label you gave the batch, when one was set.

request_status.description: Maps each custom_id to where that request currently stands.

401.description: We could not authenticate the request.

404.description: No batch exists for that ID.

500.description: Something went wrong on Valar's side.

get.summary: Get a single batch request result
get.description: Pull back the result for one request in a batch, looked up by its custom_id.

parameters.name: custom_id
parameters.description: The custom_id identifying the request inside the batch.

200.description: The response object for that request.

401.description: We could not authenticate the request.

404.description: No matching batch or request was found.

500.description: Something went wrong on Valar's side.
